output: A consumer guide document committed via PR.
context: Downstream Elyos projects and external commons need a clear guide to consuming the fan-out contract: how to parse it, required attribution, what jurisdiction means for their use case, license basis, and share-alike obligations. Without this, downstream consumers may misuse cleared items. Depends on contract-001. See PLAN.md (M2 exit, Governance).
title: Consumer guide: contract, attribution, jurisdiction, license-basis
acceptanceCriteria: The guide explains how to parse and validate the JSON-LD cleared-item contract.
Required courtesy attribution to the Library of Congress is documented with an example.
Jurisdiction scope is explained: PD in the U.S. does not mean worldwide; downstream reusers in other jurisdictions are explicitly warned to re-verify locally.
License basis is documented per rights category (CC0, CC-BY, CC-BY-SA, no-known-restrictions, pre-1929 PD).
CC-BY-SA share-alike obligations are explained with an example of propagation into derivative works.
The determination TTL field and re-verification before expiry are described.
The guide is clear enough for a downstream project to onboard without asking questions.
objective: Write the consumer guide for the cleared-item fan-out contract, covering: parsing and validation, required courtesy attribution, jurisdiction scope and warnings, license basis per rights category, CC-BY-SA share-alike propagation, and re-verification before TTL expiry.
